mechanism_narrative: A lower or falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month when policy is not tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the sign is negative in any sustained easing window longer than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid CPIAUCSL acceleration forces tighter policy expectations and higher real rates, which compress equity valuations and predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is unchanged or rising
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of inflation-surprise triggers despite rising policy-rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, so when unemployment climbs materially the index should fall over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after unemployment accelerations, or if the hit rate is not below 40% across all such trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A broad payroll expansion in PAYEMS signals stronger near-term demand and profit growth, so continued labor-market gains should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if payroll growth is followed by negative average 20-day SPX returns over rolling 2-year windows, or if the sign flips persistently after positive PAYEMS trend readings.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates firm consumer demand and housing-linked spending, which should lift cyclical earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth is positive and HOUST is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if jointly strong retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no lift over either series alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with steady or lower unemployment reflects improving production without overheating labor costs, so industrial acceleration should be associated with positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if industrial-production upswings do not coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when unemployment is not simultaneously stable or improving.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth slows while RSAFS also weakens, the economy is decelerating on both aggregate output and spending, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth decelerates and RSAFS 3-month growth turns negative
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP and retail-sales slowdowns are not followed by below-zero 60-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive in a majority of slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound after prior weakness signals a cyclical bottom in interest-sensitive demand, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next week as growth-sensitive sectors reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after a 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts rebounds do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return following the trigger is indistinguishable from zero across rolling samples.
